title: Portable Master Stream with Dual 2½″ Lines

scene.description: Engine 181 is supplying a portable master stream device with two equal 2½″ lines.

problems.title: Portable Master Stream with Dual 2½″ Lines
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a portable master stream device with two equal 2½″ lines. Portable master stream flowing 500 gpm total. Two 200′ 2½″ supply lines feeding the appliance. Each 2½″ line carries 250 gpm. Master stream nozzle pressure: 80 psi. Portable appliance loss: 25 psi. No elevation change What pump pressure should Engine 181 set?
problems.instructorExplanation: Each 2½″ line carries 250 gpm. FL per line = 2 × (250/100)² × 2 = 25 psi. PP = 25 FL + 80 NP + 25 appliance loss = 130 psi.
problems.explainMistake: For dual equal lines feeding one master stream, do not calculate each line at the full 500 gpm. Split the flow between the two equal supply lines, then use one line's FL in the final pressure calculation.

problems.title: Portable Master Stream with Dual 2½″ Lines
problems.question: Engine 181 supplies the portable master stream at 750 gpm total through two equal 200′ 2½″ lines. Each line carries 375 gpm. Master stream nozzle pressure is 80 psi and portable appliance loss is 25 psi. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = 56 FL + 80 NP + 25 appliance loss = 161 psi
problems.instructorExplanation: Split the total master stream flow between the two equal supply lines. Use one line’s friction loss in the final pressure calculation.
problems.explainMistake: For dual equal lines feeding one master stream, do not calculate each line at the full 500 gpm. Split the flow between the two equal supply lines, then use one line's FL in the final pressure calculation.

problems.title: Portable Master Stream with Dual 2½″ Lines
problems.question: The master stream is now an engine-mounted deck gun. Remove hose friction loss and portable appliance loss. Use 80 psi nozzle pressure and no elevation change. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: A deck gun mounted on the engine does not use the portable master stream supply-line friction loss or portable appliance loss.
problems.explainMistake: For dual equal lines feeding one master stream, do not calculate each line at the full 500 gpm. Split the flow between the two equal supply lines, then use one line's FL in the final pressure calculation.
